news.title: BPTP to invest ₹1,100 crore in Greater Faridabad housing project
news.para: BPTP has launched a residential project in Greater Faridabad with an estimated investment of ₹1,100 crore and a projected GDV of ₹1,800 crore. The development will add 325 premium units in the NCR market.

news.title: MMRDA secures 216 acres in Raigad for Mumbai 3.0 project through participatory model
news.para: Mumbai 3.0 project is underway with MMRDA securing 216 acres in Raigad district. This marks a significant step in a new participatory development model. Landowners are offered various compensation options. The project aims to create a new economic hub with advanced infrastructure. It is expected to generate substantial employment and attract investment, shaping a new urban future for the region.

news.title: Vedaanta Senior Living plans to invest ₹200 crore in Kerala in next three years
news.para: Vedaanta Senior Living is set to invest ₹200 crore in Kerala over the next three years, launching its Eden Gardens project in Ettumanoor with a ₹50 crore investment. This expansion includes 48 villas and 30 apartments, with prices starting at ₹76 lakh, building on their existing presence in Guruvayur and Kochi.

news.title: Office completions fall 36% quarter-on-quarter in Q1 2026 amid West Asia tensions: Report
news.para: Office space completions declined 36% quarter-on-quarter to 9.7 million sq ft in Q1 2026, while absorption rose 20% year-on-year, indicating resilient demand despite global uncertainties. Vacancy levels tightened to 9.5% as supply moderated, according to Vestian.

news.title: Ganjam administration seizes 13 tractors in drive against illegal sand mining
news.para: Ganjam district administration conducted a late-night raid, seizing 13 sand-laden tractors involved in illegal mining from the prohibited Rushikulya riverbed. This action follows the imposition of Section 163 of BNSS, prohibiting mining activities in the area. An FIR has been lodged against vehicle owners for violating prohibitory orders.

news.title: New maintenance policy for Bengaluru development body apartments begins next month
news.para: Bangalore Development Authority will soon launch a new maintenance policy for its apartment projects. This policy aims to standardize maintenance charges and clearly define developer responsibilities. It will act as an interim framework until a formal Apartment Act is notified.

news.title: Experts call for safer, planned slum housing in Lucknow
news.para: Following the Vikas Nagar fire, urban planners advocate for "in-situ redevelopment" of informal settlements to avoid disrupting residents' livelihoods. Experts stress the need for fire-resilient, low-cost housing designs with firebreak lanes and suggest "small group housing" models for vulnerable populations.

news.title: Hundreds of illegal house extensions gone in massive operation in Gurugram
news.para: Gurgaon authorities launched an intensive anti-encroachment drive on Sunday. Bulldozers cleared illegal house extensions and commercial structures across upscale colonies. Hundreds of illegal structures were demolished. This action follows a court order. The drive aims to reclaim public land and restore road widths. It will continue for the next three days.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh's draft unified building code links FAR with road width
news.para: Uttar Pradesh government has proposed a unified building code for industrial development authorities, aiming to replace disparate rules with a common framework. The draft regulations significantly increase Floor Area Ratio (FAR) limits, linking them to road widths, while reducing setback requirements and removing most height caps.

news.title: Singapore's CapitaLand Trust to divest Asia Square Tower 2 in $1.95 billion deal
news.para: Singapore's CapitaLand Integrated Commercial Trust has announced the sale of Asia Square Tower 2 for a staggering S$2.48 billion. The trust is simultaneously set to acquire the upscale Paragon mall for approximately S$3.85 billion, with the transaction for Asia Square anticipated to finalize in the latter half of 2026.

news.title: Adani Enterprises' arm sets up three hotel, real estate airport city units
news.para: Adani Airport City, a step-down wholly-owned subsidiary of the company, has set up Adani Navi Mumbai Airport City, Adani Guwahati Airport City and Adani Ahmedabad Airport City.

news.title: QXO strikes $17 billion deal to acquire building products distributor and installer TopBuild
news.para: US construction supplies distributor QXO is set to acquire building products distributor and installer TopBuild in a $17 billion deal. This acquisition will make QXO the second-largest publicly traded building products distributor in North America. The transaction has received board approval and is expected to be immediately accretive to QXO earnings.

news.title: Himachal Pradesh RERA imposes penalty on Chester Hill real estate development projects in Solan
news.para: Himachal Pradesh RERA has levied a Rs 70 lakh penalty on two real estate projects in Solan. The penalties stem from violations including irregular association formation and financial mismanagement. RERA also forwarded complaints regarding unauthorized activities and service deficiencies for further inquiry. Promoters must deposit the penalty and submit financial disclosures.

news.title: NGT forms committee to investigate encroachments at Shahpura lake in Bhopal
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has formed a committee to investigate alleged encroachments and illegal constructions around Bhopal's Shahpura lake. The tribunal is concerned about untreated water discharge, obstructed drainage, and the lake's deteriorating water quality, which is unsuitable for use. This action follows a petition highlighting the ecological damage despite ongoing beautification efforts.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh government speeds up LRS clearances ahead of April 23 deadline
news.para: The Andhra Pradesh government is pushing to approve layout regularisation applications quickly. This scheme aims to bring unauthorized layouts into the legal framework. The deadline is approaching next week. Officials are directed to clear eligible applications without delay. This initiative seeks to boost state revenue and promote planned urban development. Over 61,000 applications have been received.

news.title: Gurugram to launch doorstep demolition waste pickup, tighten site inspections
news.para: Gurugram's MCG is launching door-to-door construction and demolition waste collection and enhancing disposal methods. This initiative, alongside traffic management and dust control measures, aims to reduce the city's Air Quality Index to 170 by 2026. The civic body is also addressing legacy waste at Bandhwari landfill.

news.title: Telangana HC gives government one week to file counters in GHMC merger case
news.para: The Telangana High Court has granted the state government a final week to file its counter-affidavits challenging the merger of panchayats, municipalities, and municipal corporations into GHMC. Petitioners, including a farmer whose farmhouse was recently demolished, argue the amendments are unconstitutional and arbitrary. The court also extended the status quo on demolitions in a specific case.
